title: Fix LATCH warning on static function return values in always_comb blocks
body:
With latest version of Verilator, the following code:

```
module verilator_latch
  (
   input logic [2:0] state,
   output logic [31:0] b
   );

   function logic [31:0 ] toto () ;
      logic [31:0] res;
      res = 10;

      return res;
   endfunction

   always_comb begin
      b = 0;
      if(state != 2) begin
         b = toto();
      end
   end

endmodule;
```

Gives the following warning:
```
verilator --no-timing --lint-only -Wall verilator_latch.sv
%Warning-LATCH: verilator_latch.sv:14:4: Latch inferred for signal 'verilator_latch.toto__Vstatic__res' (not all control paths of combinational always assign a value)
                                       : ... Suggest use of always_latch for intentional latches
   14 |    always_comb begin
      |    ^~~~~~~~~~~
                ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/LATCH?v=5.010
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off LATCH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

There is no actual latch in this code so this seems to be a bug to me.
